title: Saudi Falcon Arabic LLM Program
entry_mechanism: SDAIA-led national programme with compute infrastructure from Aramco/STC and Arabic data from MOE/MOH/government corpus
executive_summary:
Arabic is spoken by 420 million people and is the official language of 22 countries, yet the global AI large language model landscape is dominated by English-first systems. GPT-4, Claude, and Gemini have poor Arabic capabilities — generating grammatical errors, missing dialectal nuance, and struggling with right-to-left text in mixed documents. Saudi Arabia has a strategic opportunity to develop the world's premier Arabic-language large language model: a national AI capability that serves Saudi government, enterprise, and consumer applications while establishing Saudi Arabia as the AI hub of the Arabic-speaking world.

Node DG-1 targets the Saudi Falcon LLM Programme: developing and deploying a sovereign Arabic LLM with state-of-the-art performance on Arabic NLP benchmarks, deployed on Saudi sovereign infrastructure, and commercially licensed to Saudi government and GCC enterprise customers. The UAE's Falcon LLM (by TIIUAE) demonstrates the model — the Saudi version should be specifically optimised for the Gulf Arabic dialect ecosystem and government use cases.

The global LLM market was valued at $6.4 billion in 2024 growing at 79% CAGR. A sovereign Saudi Arabic LLM licensed to Saudi and GCC government agencies, Arabic media companies, Arabic e-commerce platforms, and Arabic-language education providers creates a large and defensible revenue opportunity.

companies.name: Hugging Face
companies.rationale: Hugging Face provides the open-source ML infrastructure (Transformers library) and hosts models. Saudi Falcon could be developed on HF infrastructure and published on HF Hub.

companies.name: Cohere
companies.rationale: Cohere builds enterprise LLMs with sovereign deployment capability. Cohere has Middle East government engagement and a flexible deployment model.

companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.rationale: NVIDIA provides the H100/H200 GPU clusters required for LLM training. NVIDIA has active Saudi Arabia engagement through the DGX SuperPOD programme.

companies.name: Microsoft Azure OpenAI
companies.rationale: Microsoft has Azure Saudi Arabia regions and OpenAI partnership. Hybrid deployment of Saudi Falcon on Azure sovereign regions.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.rationale: Mistral provides open-weight models that can be fine-tuned on Arabic data. European technology with sovereign deployment model.

partners.name: SDAIA (Saudi Data and AI Authority)
partners.current: National AI strategy; some NLP research; Arabic NLP standards
partners.upgrade: Lead Saudi Falcon LLM programme; establish Saudi AI computing cluster (1,000+ H100s); Arabic corpus curation from government and public sources; programme management and IP ownership
partners.timeline: 24 months to first public Saudi Falcon release

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Digital
partners.current: NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD; large-scale AI compute; industrial AI applications
partners.upgrade: Aramco compute cluster available for Saudi Falcon training; Aramco Arabic technical corpus contribution; Aramco as anchor enterprise customer for Saudi Falcon

partners.name: KAUST AI Research Centre
partners.current: NLP research; Arabic language processing; LLM research; some Arabic NLP datasets
partners.upgrade: KAUST as Saudi Falcon research engine; Arabic benchmark development; Gulf dialect adaptation research; KAUST researchers as model development core team

partners.name: Ministry of Education / Arabic Content
partners.current: Massive Arabic language corpus (textbooks, curriculum, assessments)
partners.upgrade: MOE Arabic educational corpus for LLM training; Saudi Falcon integration into Saudi school AI assistant; Arabic educational AI anchor use case
